findings.evidence: Line 39 defines `condExp_blockIndicatorProd_ae_eq_prod`; lines 43-44 show the required `iCondIndepFun ... (tailProcess X)` hypothesis. Mathlib naming says hypotheses, when needed, are separated by `of`; nearby `Exchangeable.blockLaw_eq_prefixLaw_of_injective` names its extra hypothesis.
findings.issue: `condExp_blockIndicatorProd_ae_eq_prod` names only the `=ᵐ` product conclusion, but the statement depends on `hCI : iCondIndepFun ... (tailProcess X) ...`; without an `_of_...` suffix it advertises an unconditional conditional-expectation factorization.

findings.evidence: Line 76 defines `blockLaw_eq_lintegral_prod_directingMeasure`; lines 80-81 show the required `iCondIndepFun ... (tailProcess X)` hypothesis, while the capstone at line 117 correctly uses `conditionallyIID_of_iCondIndepFun_tailProcess`.
findings.issue: `blockLaw_eq_lintegral_prod_directingMeasure` likewise omits the required tail conditional-independence hypothesis, so the name overstates the theorem as a general block-law mixture identity.

summary: Two new theorem names omit the essential conditional-independence hypothesis, making the API read as unconditional factorization facts. Rename them with an `_of_iCondIndepFun_tailProcess` suffix or equivalent conclusion-first `..._of_...` wording.
